Resource title: Complete Guide to Calculus Foundations for Data Science
Stated learning outcomes:
Evaluate limits of various functions, including polynomial, rational, trigonometric, logarithmic, and exponential functions, using multiple techniques such as direct substitution, factoring, and the squeeze theorem.
    Demonstrate proficiency in finding the derivatives of common functions and applying differentiation rules—including the product, quotient, chain, and power rules—to efficiently calculate derivatives.
    Analyze the behavior of functions using derivatives, including finding extreme values, applying the Mean Value Theorem, solving optimization and related rates problems, and using techniques such as linear approximations, L'Hôpital's Rule, and Newton's Method.
    Master integration techniques, such as substitution, integration by parts, and partial fraction decomposition, to evaluate both indefinite and definite integrals of various function types.
    Apply integral calculus to solve real-world problems, including calculating areas between curves, volumes of solids, arc lengths, and solving problems related to work, energy, and probability.
Stated prerequisites: First, it is important to have a basic knowledge of arithmetic and algebra to know how to work with equations, functions, and inequalities. Second, it is helpful to have a basic knowledge of geometry, such as basic shapes and properties, along with trigonometry and its various functions. This knowledge of mathematics will help you perform the calculations throughout this course. If you have taken a pre-calculus or calculus course in the past, this course will bring you up to speed.
Intended audience: This course is an ideal fit for data scientists and data professionals seeking to advance their knowledge of the tools used in their profession and develop a deeper understanding of the mathematical science that drives the world of data.
Skill area: Demonstrate proficiency in mathematics, including calculus, linear algebra, probability and statistics, and discrete mathematics.

Stated learning outcomes:
By the end of this course, the learner will be able to:
Compare the evolution of telecom networks from 2G to 6G, including key architectural differences, capabilities, and standards defined by 3GPP and ITU.
Explain the key components and functions of 4G (LTE) and 5G network architectures, including RAN, core network (AMF, SMF, and UPF), and transport network.
Analyze spectrum allocation, frame structures, and physical layer technologies—including OFDMA, MIMO, and carrier aggregation—used in 4G and 5G networks.
Interpret call flows and signaling procedures in 4G and 5G networks, including registration, mobility management, and end-to-end session establishment.
Evaluate emerging technologies such as virtualization, cloud computing, and network slicing in the context of 5G and future 6G networks.

Resource title: Additive Manufacturing: Testing and Simulating 3D Prints
Stated learning outcomes:
Understanding 3D Printing Processes: Learn about the entire process from design to validation, including simulation, fabrication, and testing of 3D prints.
Simulation and Testing: Explore how to simulate 3D printed parts, optimize designs, and test both plastic and metal 3D prints.
Standards and Validation: Get insights into evolving standards for additive manufacturing and methods for validating low-cost filament prints and qualifying materials.
Skill area: Other (Specify in the 'Comments' column)
Comments: Understanding 3D Printing Processes

Resource title: PLC Simulation Software: Factory I/O with Connected Component Workbench
Stated learning outcomes:
PLC Programming: Learn to program PLCs using simulation software, which is essential for those without access to actual PLCs.
Simulation Software: Gain hands-on experience with Connected Components Workbench (CCW) and Factory I/O software through progressively complex projects.
Practical Projects: Work on real-world projects such as programming a control panel, a conveyor belt, a filling tank with a timer, and sorting boxes by height.
Troubleshooting Tips: Learn useful troubleshooting tips for CCW software and Factory I/O to enhance your problem-solving skills.
Stated prerequisites: Before starting this course, you should be familiar with ladder logic programming, including basic instructions like input and output instructions, and how timer instructions work. If you need a refresher or have no experience with ladder logic programming, it is recommended to check out the course 'Learning PLC Ladder Logic' available in the library.
Skill area: Other (Specify in the 'Comments' column)
Comments: Learn to program PLCs using simulation software

Stated learning outcomes:
Learning objectives:
Review the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and five associated themes.
Learn about the people theme and SDGs 1–5: no poverty, zero hunger, good health and well-being, quality education, and gender equality.
Explore a real-world case study on creating education solutions for refugee children in Jordan (SDGs 4, 10).
Learn about the planet theme and SDGs 6, 12–14: responsible consumption and production, climate action, and life below water.
Explore a real-world case study on protecting mangroves in Malaysia (SDGs 13, 15).
Learn about the prosperity theme and SDGs 7–10: affordable and clean energy; decent work and economic growth; industry, innovation, and infrastructure; and reduced inequalities.
Explore a real-world case study covering the smart tech partnership in Dubai (SDGs 9, 11, 17).
Learn about the peace theme and SDG 16: peace, justice, and strong institutions.
Explore a real-world case study covering birth registrations in Pakistan (SDG 10, 16, 17).
Learn about the partnership theme and SDG 17: partnerships for the goals.
Explore a real-world case study about a Kenyan mobile network operator delivering innovative solutions to customers (8, 9, 17).
Discover how you can contribute to SDGs and create a more sustainable future.
Skill area: Other (Specify in the 'Comments' column)
Comments: Clear and practical introduction to how technology can accelerate progress toward the SDGs. Skill areas covered: SDG, Technology Education
